questions.text: The number of zeroes of a quadratic polynomial is at most:
questions.explanation: A quadratic polynomial (degree 2) has at most 2 zeroes. These are the x-coordinates where the graph intersects the x-axis.

questions.text: If α and β are zeroes of ax^2 + bx + c, then α + β = -b/a.
questions.explanation: For a quadratic polynomial ax^2 + bx + c, sum of zeroes (α + β) = -b/a and product (αβ) = c/a.

questions.text: A polynomial with degree 3 can have at most ________ zeroes.
questions.explanation: A polynomial of degree n has at most n zeroes. So a cubic polynomial (degree 3) has at most 3 zeroes.

questions.text: If one zero of x^2 - 5x + 6 is 2, find the other zero.
questions.explanation: x^2 - 5x + 6 = (x-2)(x-3). If one zero is 2, the other zero is 3. Or use: sum of zeroes = 5, so other = 5-2 = 3.
